response: The internet’s origins trace back to 1960s US Department of Defense–funded research, culminating in the ARPANET demonstration between UCLA and Stanford in 1969 and the adoption of TCP/IP in 1983 to form today’s network backbone. Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (HTML, URLs, HTTP) and the release of the Mosaic browser in 1993 opened the web to ordinary users, fueling the late-1990s dot-com boom and the 2001 crash that left only giants like Amazon and Google standing. The mid-2000s rise of social media platforms (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube), the launch of AWS in 2006, and the iPhone in 2007 ushered in ubiquitous mobile access and cloud computing. Today, over 5 billion people rely on the internet for commerce, communication, and entertainment.
